title: Uvifera ramosissima
taxonomy: Uvifera ramosissima is classified under the plant family Urticaceae. This plant family, which also includes nettles and other related species, is commonly known as the nettle family. Uvifera ramosissima is often placed within the genus Uvifera, although some sources may group it differently. Further classification involves assigning the plant to a specific species, which may be subject to ongoing research or variation depending on the source. Overall, the classification of Uvifera ramosissima reflects its botanical relationships with other plants within the Urticaceae family.
uses: Uvifera ramosissima, also known as the ramified uva, is a plant species with various traditional uses, including medicinal applications. Its leaves, bark, and roots are used in folk medicine to treat different health conditions. Uvifera ramosissima is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses.
